Keeping a track of Community based organization’s (CBO) fund, 
cash flow across various entities and the degree of fund utilization is a key aspect for organizations working for the upliftment of the underprivileged section of the society. Any discrepancy or mistakes in the fund management process doesn’t only affects the overall planning and operation of the organization but also affects the recipients of the funds. Manual system for maintaining account books are inefficient, time consuming and prone to human errors thereby impacting the overall mission and vision of the organization involved in the betterment of the underprivileged sections.
Techno Brain proposed the adaptation of a web and mobile 
based accounting system to automate existing manual processes and provide a robust and effective solution to computerize the accounting system at Village Organizations, Mandal Samakhyas & Zilla Samakhyas level. The proposed solution will allow the field users to carry out their daily operations with utmost ease and efficiency. The solution will address the need of having greater transparency and standardization and enable the stakeholders to make quick and informed decisions based on their daily operations and real time information available in the system. The system will ensure effective monitoring of funds across various entities while providing strict control on various transactions done within the application, thereby enhancing the credibility of the users as well as of the organization as a whole.1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
PAGE 1Techno Brain is a leading provider of technical solutions to government ministries, local governments, private sector across the globe with over a decade of experience globally. With our extensive experience in providing accounting solutions which are driven by industry standard software development practices, CMMi level 3, we bring you solutions that are holistic and robust. Our accounting solution encompass a complete structure of detailed analysis of your existing systems, strategic goals, and futuristic envisioning. Supported by our pool of domain and technology experts, we bring business-focused solutions that offer the highest Return of Investment.
The proposed solution will allow the field users to carry out their daily operations with utmost ease and efficiency

Traditional pen and paper field accounting system require a 
large amount of back office support. Field workers enter the accounting details in the forms while in the field and then the gathered information is entered into a filing system back at the office. This file process often includes transcribing the accounting information into digital format, associating required information, and organizing the results for follow up actions. In addition this type of manual process often yield non- uniform results, are labor intensive, and are susceptible to error. The major challenges faced in a manual accounting system are:
• Maintaining large volumes of financial transactions in manual 
books of accounts is cumbersome. As the accounting practices and treatment vary across various livelihoods, financial and social development activities, the manual accounting system becomes complex over a period
• Manual generation of MIS reports such as Balance Sheet, Receipts & Payments and Income & Expenditure can be time consuming and prone to manual errors
• Manual maintenance of Program grants
• Coordinating with banks and other institutions is challenging and are subject to human
• errors. Business can see itself in fines and penalties if records are lost.
• Manual records are easier to be falsified, modified, altered or vanished, as compared to
• computerized records which become very safe when using passwords, firewalls, and back-ups.
• Lots of manual labour is required for record updating, maintaining and keeping.
• Department stakeholders are not able to monitor and provide effective facilitation and
• nurturing support to CBOs.2. INTRODUCTION
PAGE 2With a Web and Mobile based accounting system, field workers can capture and transfer the information captured in real time back to the office. This process used eliminates the need for back office support to perform daily accounting activities because the data is collected in the proper standardized format directly in the field. This digital field data can be used to generate reports, keep a track of money flow and its utilization at various levels.
In this document we will be discussing the Mobile and Web based 
accounting system in depth and its uses at Village, Mandal and Samakhyas level.
...field workers can capture and transfer the information captured in real time back to the office

PAGE 3
Techno Brain, with its vast experience 
in providing solutions to governments is a proposing a connected government solution through various technologies such as web portals and mobile applications. The solution has been designed specifically for the business of government, meaning that the features and functionality available in the solution address the challenges that have been discussed in the previous section. The solution is designed on highly valued principles such as the need to be agile, intuitive and extremely powerful to handle vast amounts of data.
The proposed accounting system is 
developed on Android based Tablets for Village Organization (VO) and Web based application on window’s operating systems for Mandal Samakhyas (MSs) and Zilla Samakhyas (ZSs) [i.e., VO is Tablet application, MS and ZS are Web applications]. The solution enables the VO, MS and ZS to improve Financial, 3. Solution Overview
Management and Non-Financial activities viz., savings mobilizations, lending to member organizations, micro-insurance & pension and livelihood activities. The application works in off-line and on-line mode for VO Accounting as it is developed as Tablet application.
The key features of the application are
• Multilingual support (English and Local language) for screens; data will be displayed in English.
• All the transactional data of VO, MS and ZS accounting are sent to the central server for generating Financial Reports like Balance Sheet, Income & Expenditure, Receipts & Payments and MIS reports like Start up Balances status, Regularity of Meetings with respective percentages.
• Application covers financial transactions of VOs, MSs and ZS & seamless integration of inter and intra transactions at all levels.
• Flexibility to add feature projects directly into existing application without modifications.
• The software enable CBOs to carryout accounting on a day to day transaction basis in double entry system, for the all thematic areas like savings, credit, borrowings, insurance, CIF and other borrowed funds
The solution is designed on highly valued principles such as the need to be agile, intuitive and extremely powerful to handle vast amounts of data.

PAGE 4
The Mobile Accounting System consists 
of accounting activities three Village Organization, Cluster Level Federation and Block Level Federation. The application consists of• Tablet and Web Based Accounting System at Village Organization
• Web Based Accounting System at Mandal Samakhyas and Zilla 
Samakhyas
     4.1 VillageOrganization
The Mobile Accounting system consists of 
the following modules at VO level
Application Settings: The application 
setting module is used to define the parameters required for running the application. The following features are covered under this module
• Meeting Settings: This screen allows 
the VO user to set the number of meetings to be conducted in a month and its date. The settings are applicable for the next 6 months.
Masters: The master module is used to 
define the master data that is required to populate a form or perform data entry tasks. The following features are covered under this module
• Bank Accounts Settings: Each VO has 
Bank accounts operated for different components like CIF, Sthreenidhi, POP , SGSY and HN etc. All the Bank Details along with component name is captured for carrying out various transactions.
• VO Profile: This screen shows profile of VO like VO ID, VO Registration No, VO Name, VO Address and contact details etc.
Start Up Form: The Start Up form is used 
to capture the closing balance of the last year in the system. Once the last year’s closing balance is captured in the system, the same will be reflected as opening balance for the current financial year.4. Application Modules
• Account Balances: The last financial year Balance Sheet is entered for each component in this screen.
•  Account Information: All transactions at VO level are divided into 3 types.
 o Savings: Generally SHG’s will 
save savings in VO for VO sustainability. It allows entering all SHG’s wise saving     details.
 o Loans: VO will give different 
Loans to SHG’s and SHG’s will replay amount to VO. To understand all SHG’s       individual Loan outstanding’s 
like Demand, Collection and Balance need to enter in this screen. o Others: To take membership 
in VO, SHG has to pay Entrance Fee, Membership Fee and Share Capital. All       transactions between VO and 
SHG can be recorded in this screen.
Meetings: The meeting module is used to 
carry out the various financial transactions 
at VO Level. The following features are covered under this module
• New Meeting: All financial transactions 
are carried out through meetings at VO level. A new meeting has to be initiated every month to capture all the financial transactions. The following are the different transactions at VO level.
 o SHG Attendance
 o Prayer
 o Agenda
 o Staff Attendance
 o Visitors Attendance
 o Receipts: Receipts are divided 
into 3 categories: SHG to VO, MS to VO and Others to VO
• o Payments: Payments are divided into 
3 categories: VO to SHG, VO to MS and  VO to Others 
  o Journal  o Contra Entry
  o Resolutions
• Search Meeting: This feature enables 
the user to search an edit an existing meeting which is in progress.
• Reports: The following reports are generated in the Meetings module.
o Balance Sheet: It is final report of the accounting where assets and liabilities xwill be displayed. We can decide VO health based on this report.o Receipts Payments: It shows VO all receipts and payments in the system. All grants which are coming from the MS will be displayed as Receipt and all expenditures will be displayed at Payment side.o Income Expenditure: Income Expenditure shows profit or loss of a VO.o Loan DCB: Loan DCB shows each SHG Loan details with Demand, Collection and Balance
Final accounting Reports: The 
Accounting reports covered under this 
module are listed below
• Balance Sheet
• Receipts payments
• Income Expenditure
• Cashbook Report
• Bankbook Report
• Ledger Details
• Dashboard
• Trail Balance
Trade Accounting: The trading module 
provides the following features:
• Stock purchase / sales: All VO 
purchases and sales transactions are captured in this
• screen.
• Stock Ledger: It shows current stock at VO level.
• Trading Account Report: It shows final report of the trading account which profit or
• loss.
Work Activity: The Work Activity module 
provides the following features:• Monthly Work Activity
• Work Activity
Leave• Staff Leave: This screen is used by staff at VO level to apply for leaves
• Staff Leave Report: The report provides the list of staff on leave
Staff Attendance• Staff Attendance: It allows to capture the attendance of the staff at VO level

PAGE 7

• Elimination of Manual books and duplication of work and one time data capture
• Electronic accounting system with internal control for effective monitoring of funds
• Real-time & accurate Online data availability at all VOs, MSs and ZSs
• Integration of accounting data with SHGs and across other CBO entities
6. Benefits of Mobile Accounting Solution
7. Conclusion
The Web and mobile based accounting system is an effetive medium to reduce the over head involved in a manual 
accounting system and to eliminate the duplicacy of work, manul errors and fraud in the system. The system streamlines the entire accounting process at VO.MS and ZS level and enhances productivity, accoutability and transperacny in the system, thereby enabling the state government to provide quick and efficient services to the targeted groups.
PAGE 8
